options: ones ▲ 下篇短文共有 7 個空格,為第 26 至 32 題,請依短文文意,選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 It’s Christmas, one of the most fantastic holidays in a year. People are busy decorating Christmas trees and 26 gifts for families and friends. Golden lights shine in the house and the air 27 the smell of roasted chicken and pudding. Also, you can hear people around you 28 Christmas songs like “Silent Nights”, and “Wish you a Merry Christmas”. It’s a magic moment that everyone looks forward to. Every year, Germany’s most famous Nuremberg Market opens its stalls for visitors from all over the world from November 28th to December 24th. In the medieval atmosphere, people can enjoy their perfect Christmas holidays. Here, you can buy all kinds of handmade wooden toys, tree decorations and souvenirs. Among these items is one that the market is particularly known 29 —prune dolls. 30 with dried fruit and nuts, these dolls are to keep away evil spirits and bring joy to the house. 31 high point of the market is the various kinds of baked goods, such as Guelph puddings and gingerbread cookies. Anyone who goes to Nuremberg is sure to leave 32 a smile and a warm feeling.

options: with 四、閱讀測驗 ( 第 33-40 題,每題 2 分,共 16 分 ) 說明:以下有兩篇短文,共有 8 個題目,為第 33 至 40 題,請於閱讀短文後,選出最適當的答案 。 ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 33-36 題。 On August 11, 2014, the news of the Hollywood superstar Robin Williams’ death shocked the world. This sixty-three-year-old actor was found dead in his home. An autopsy was planned later, but authorities believed that Williams killed himself. He is survived by his wife and three children. The Marin County Sheriff’s Office recounted that they received a 911 call from Williams’ residence at 11:55 a.m. reporting the actor had been found unconscious and was not breathing. According to Robin’s publicist, who confirmed the news, Robin suffered from depression and struggled with drug and alcohol addiction for much of his career. Robin, born in Chicago, was an outstanding actor and a comedian. Starting as a stand-up comedian, he soon rose to fame as Mork, an alien on earth, in the TV series Mork & Mindy. He took his career to the next level with his extraordinary talent shown in Good Morning Vietnam, Dead Poets Society, and The Fisher King, all of which garnered him Oscar nominations for Best Actor. But it wasn’t until his performance in “ Good Will Huntin g” that he earned his only win as Best Supporting Actor. Williams also scored in another dramatic role as a man of medicine in the 1990 movie Awakenings based on neuroscientist Oliver Sacks’ memoir. In Patch Adams, Robin was a caring doctor who would do everything to cheer up his patients. In tears, Robin’s fans can’t enjoy his unique way of performing anymore. His last film, Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb will be released in December, 2014. Quite a few characters Robin played in the movies were positive. However, he ended his life tragically. Like his wife Susan Schneider said in a statement,“......it is our hope that the focus will not be on Robin’s death, but on the countless moments of joy and laughter Robin gave to millions.” 33. Which of the following is true? (A) Robin spent many years trying to become successful as an actor. (B) His latest movie would be “Night at the Museum”. (C) He won Academy Award for The Best Actor in 1992. (D) He drank to death. 34. Which movie helped Robin win the Academy Award for the Best Supporting Actor? (A) Awakening (B) Good Will Hunting (C) Patch Adams (D) Good Morning, Vietnam

questions.q: Which of the following is true?
options: Robin spent many years trying to become successful as an actor.
His latest movie would be “Night at the Museum”.
He won Academy Award for The Best Actor in 1992.

options: He was hit by a truck on the street.
He died of a heart attack.

options: regulated ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 37-40 題 The Ebola outbreak in West Africa is the world’s deadliest to date and the World Health Organization has declared an international health emergency. Ebola virus disease (EVD) is a disease of humans and other primates caused by an Ebola virus. Symptoms such as a fever, sore throat, muscle pain and headaches start two days to three weeks after one contracts the virus. Typically, vomiting and diarrhea follow, along with decreased functioning of the liver and kidneys. Around this time, the infected may begin to bleed both within the body and externally. The virus may be acquired upon contact with blood or bodily fluids of an infected animal. In Africa, fruit bats are considered possible natural hosts of Ebola virus. They are believed to carry and spread the virus even without being infected. The monkeys were also observed to be carrying the virus. Spreading through the air has not been documented in the natural environment. Once human infection occurs, the disease may spread between people as well. Prevention includes decreasing the spread of the disease from infected animals to humans. This may be done by checking the infected animals, killing them and properly disposing of the bodies if the disease is discovered. Cooking meat properly and wearing protective clothing when handling meat may also be helpful, as are wearing protective clothing and washing hands when around a person with the disease. Banning or restricting the movement of animals from infected farms to other areas might reduce the spread of the disease. No specific treatment for the disease is yet available, but new drug therapies are being evaluated. The disease has a high mortality rate, often killing between 50% and 90% of those infected with the virus.EVD was first identified in Sudan and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. From 1976 (when it was first identified) through 2013, fewer than 1,000 people per year were infected. The largest outbreak to date is the ongoing 2014 West Africa Ebola outbreak, which is affecting Guinea, Sierra Leone, Liberia and Nigeria. As of August 2014, more than 1,750 suspected cases have been reported. Efforts are going on to develop a vaccine. Before any effective medical treatment can be done to those infected, people in Africa can’t do anything but wait.

options: Ebola-infected people would die soon within twenty-four hours.

It is only by contacting the infected animals that infection of people occurs. 第貳部分:非選擇題 ( 第 1 至 5 題,每題 4 分,共 20 分 ) 一、填充 說明:請依據中文之提示,在空格內填入適當之詞彙以完成句子。每格限填一字,並將空格內之 完整詞彙依序寫在「答案卷」之指定範圍內。
